Title: Engineered plant control of associative nitrogen fixation
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only a subpopulation of root-colonizing cells responded in situ, indicating signaling remained suboptimal.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhances rhizopine perception and intracellular accumulation when combined with IntBC, enabling host-specific activation of downstream nitrogen fixation circuitry
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant-derived transkingdom signal used to control bacterial gene expression in association with the host plant.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MocB-mediated rhizopine perception drove PmocB-dependent reporter expression. 2. Coexpression with IntBC improved SI sensitivity by at least 10^3-fold in A. caulinodans. 3. This hybrid uptake system enabled plant-dependent activation of bacterial nitrogen fixation genes on RhiP barley roots.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PJ23115 constitutive promoter in pSIR02 for mocB-intBC; PmocB inducible promoter as readout
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed from engineered plasmid in A. caulinodans during in vitro culture and root colonization.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SI supplementation; association with rhizopine-producing barley
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude IntBC functions as transmembrane and ABC components of a hybrid SI uptake system utilizing MocB as the solute-binding protein.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reporter assays; flow cytometry; intracellular SI bioassay; in situ root colonization imaging
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central component for building highly sensitive rhizopine biosensors in associative diazotrophs.

Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In situ signaling still activated only a minority of cells on roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes SI uptake and increases sensitivity/range of rhizopine-inducible expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a host-derived signal to control bacterial gene expression and nitrogen fixation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. intBC were required for sensitive transcriptional response to SI in R. leguminosarum tests. 2. Coexpression of intBC with mocB in A. caulinodans improved SI perception by at least 10^3-fold. 3. The resulting hybrid uptake system enabled rhizopine-dependent activation on barley roots.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Plac constitutive promoter in Rlv test; PJ23115 constitutive promoter in pSIR02
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed from engineered plasmid in A. caulinodans.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SI supplementation; myo-inositol-induced native intABC expression in Rlv; association with RhiP barley
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interpreted as part of hybrid SI uptake system with MocB as SBP.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native intABC induced by myo-inositol in Rlv
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant comparison in Rlv; constitutive complementation; reporter assays; SI bioassay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transporter engineering target for increasing rhizopine sensitivity in bacterial inoculants.

Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In situ signaling still activated only a minority of cells on roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes SI uptake and increases sensitivity/range of rhizopine-inducible expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a host-derived signal to control bacterial gene expression and nitrogen fixation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. intBC were required for sensitive transcriptional response to SI in R. leguminosarum tests. 2. Coexpression of intBC with mocB in A. caulinodans improved SI perception by at least 10^3-fold. 3. The resulting hybrid uptake system enabled rhizopine-dependent activation on barley roots.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Plac constitutive promoter in Rlv test; PJ23115 constitutive promoter in pSIR02
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed from engineered plasmid in A. caulinodans.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SI supplementation; myo-inositol-induced native intABC expression in Rlv; association with RhiP barley
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interpreted as part of hybrid SI uptake system with MocB as SBP.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native intABC induced by myo-inositol in Rlv
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant comparison in Rlv; constitutive complementation; reporter assays; SI bioassay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transporter engineering target for increasing rhizopine sensitivity in bacterial inoculants.

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive overexpression of SI biosynthesis genes may cause defects in plant metabolism, influence biomass accumulation, and enrich specialized SI-catabolizing bacteria in the field.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product as part of engineered rhizopine biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering host plants to produce rhizopine and selectively control associated diazotroph traits; expression should be tuned to the minimum level required for bacterial response.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root-associated interaction in barley seedlings; rhizosphere/root colonization context
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: scyllo-inosamine pathway intermediate / contributes to SI production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized R. leguminosarum idhA was introduced into barley as part of an engineered rhizopine biosynthesis module. 2. Together with mosB, it enabled rhizopine production in homozygous T2 RhiP barley. 3. Plant-produced rhizopine activated bacterial rhizopine perception and downstream nitrogen fixation control specifically on RhiP barley roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in barley from the Zea mays ubiquitin1 constitutive promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-MS metabolite profiling of rhizopine in 10-d-old seedlings; raw data in Datasets S3 and S4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in engineered rhizopine biosynthesis leading to production of scyllo-inosamine in barley.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression in barley; GC-MS metabolite analysis; bacterial biosensor response on roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a plant-derived bacterial signal that confers host-specific control of bacterial gene expression and nitrogen fixation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene was previously reported in the authors' earlier work and reused here in a homozygous T2 barley line.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes rhizopine production in barley roots, enabling host-dependent activation of bacterial nitrogen fixation circuitry

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive overexpression of SI biosynthesis genes may cause defects in plant metabolism, influence biomass accumulation, and enrich specialized SI-catabolizing bacteria in the field.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product as part of engineered rhizopine biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for engineering rhizopine-producing cereals that can selectively control associative diazotrophs.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root-associated interaction in barley seedlings; rhizosphere/root colonization context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized S. meliloti L5-30 mosB was introduced into barley together with idhA. 2. The engineered pathway produced SI in homozygous T2 RhiP barley roots. 3. Plant-derived SI was sufficient to induce bacterial signaling and host-dependent nitrogen fixation traits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in barley from the Oryza sativa ubiquitin1 constitutive promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-MS metabolite profiling of rhizopine in 10-d-old seedlings; raw data in Datasets S3 and S4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in engineered rhizopine biosynthesis to generate the rhizopine signal scyllo-inosamine in barley.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression in barley; GC-MS metabolite analysis; bacterial biosensor response on roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a plant-derived bacterial signal that confers host-specific control of bacterial gene expression and nitrogen fixation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene was previously reported in the authors' earlier work and reused here in a homozygous T2 barley line.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes rhizopine production in barley roots, enabling host-dependent activation of bacterial nitrogen fixation circuitry

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ammonia derived from biological nitrogen fixation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master activator required to drive nitrogenase gene expression; rhizopine-dependent nifA expression was sufficient to switch on nifH expression and nitrogenase activity specifically on RhiP barley.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: rhizopine/scyllo-inosamine sensed through MocR-PmocB circuitry; low oxygen diazotrophic conditions; ammonium status influences activity context
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: N-free diazotrophic conditions; 3% O2 in vitro; 1% O2 on roots; rhizopine supplementation or rhizopine-producing barley; NH4Cl repression context tested
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: rpoN; nifH; nitrogenase gene expression module
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed from rhizopine-inducible promoter PmocB in engineered bacteria during association with RhiP barley roots.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both wild-type nifA and mutant nifAL94Q/D95Q were tested; the mutant conferred stronger activity under ammonium-replete conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with sigma factor RpoN to activate nitrogenase gene transcription
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides biologically fixed nitrogen to support plant nutrition/agricultural productivity.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates nifH and other nitrogenase genes via nifA-rpoN cassette
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for synthetic symbiosis design; placing nifA under host-derived rhizopine control enables host-specific nitrogen fixation, though optimization is needed for stronger in situ activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes host-dependent activation of nitrogenase expression and activity
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suboptimal in situ nitrogenase activity and mild colonization defect in engineered strain background; partial activity can still occur in ammonium with mutant nifA cassette.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native nitrogen-fixing conditions in A. caulinodans; exact pattern not detailed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: markerless nifA deletion mutant; rhizopine-inducible complementation/controller plasmids; PnifH reporter assay; acetylene reduction assay; confocal microscopy; flow cytometry
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rhizopine-inducible nifA together with rpoN restored nifH expression and nitrogenase activity in a nifA deletion mutant under diazotrophic conditions. 2. No nitrogenase activity was detected without rhizopine, demonstrating tight binary control. 3. On RhiP barley roots, nifA-dependent nitrogenase activation occurred specifically on rhizopine-producing plants but remained suboptimal relative to wild type.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ammonia derived from biological nitrogen fixation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential N-metabolism sigma factor coexpressed with nifA to drive nitrogenase expression under rhizopine control.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: coexpressed with nifA from rhizopine-inducible PmocB circuitry; low oxygen diazotrophic context
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: N-free diazotrophic conditions; 3% O2 in vitro; 1% O2 on roots; rhizopine supplementation or rhizopine-producing barley; NH4Cl tested
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: nifH and nitrogenase regulon in conjunction with NifA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed from rhizopine-inducible promoter PmocB in engineered bacteria during association with RhiP barley roots.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed only as part of nifA-rpoN cassettes, not independently separated in most functional tests.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Works functionally with NifA and RNA polymerase holoenzyme
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides biologically fixed nitrogen to support plant nutrition/agricultural productivity.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation support via sigma-54-dependent promoter recognition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: enables activation of nifH and related genes when coexpressed with nifA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary companion regulator in host-gated nitrogen fixation modules for associative diazotroph engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables rhizopine-dependent activation of nitrogenase expression and activity when coexpressed with nifA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineered system still showed suboptimal in situ activity compared with wild type.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native nitrogen-fixing conditions in A. caulinodans; exact pattern not detailed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: rhizopine-inducible nifA-rpoN cassette assays; PnifH reporter assay; acetylene reduction assay; in situ root colonization assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rhizopine-inducible rpoN coexpressed with nifA was required to activate nitrogenase gene expression in engineered A. caulinodans. 2. The nifA-rpoN cassette conferred binary rhizopine-dependent nitrogenase activity in vitro. 3. In situ activation occurred specifically on rhizopine-producing barley roots.
